title: REET 2026 — Rajasthan Teacher Eligibility Test
meta_description: REET 2026 Level 1 (Classes 1–5) & Level 2 (6–8). Lifetime validity. 7,700+ vacancies. No negative marking. 60% to pass.

hero.one_liner: Rajasthan teacher eligibility test — mandatory for government school teaching in Rajasthan. Level 1 (primary) and Level 2 (upper primary).

what_is_it: REET (Rajasthan Eligibility Examination for Teachers) conducted by Board of Secondary Education, Rajasthan (BSER) certifies eligibility for government school teaching. Level 1: Classes 1–5 (primary). Level 2: Classes 6–8 (upper primary). One of India's most competitive teacher exams — 30+ lakh applicants annually, larger than many national exams. Passing REET is first gate; subsequent Rajasthan 3rd Grade Teacher recruitment exam determines actual appointment.

REET comprises 150 MCQs in 2.5 hours with NO negative marking — attempt all questions. Syllabus: Child Development & Pedagogy (30 Qs), Language 1 & 2 (60 Qs), Mathematics (30 Qs), EVS/Social Studies (30 Qs). Qualifying marks: 60% for General (90/150), 55% for OBC/SC/ST. Lifetime validity (changed from 3-year limit in 2021) is major advantage — pass once, appear for recruitment exams anytime in future.

For Rajasthan residents, particularly those with teaching aptitude, REET is a direct pathway to secure government job (salary ₹28,000–55,000/month depending on level, plus pension). Teachers command social respect in Rajasthani society. Teaching also allows work-life balance (regular school hours, 2-month summer vacation). Competition is fierce because of these factors — but serious preparation over 4–6 months positions you in successful band (100+ out of 150).

key_details.label: Negative Marking
key_details.value: No negative marking — no penalty for wrong

exam_pattern.description: Single paper, 150 questions, 150 marks, 150 minutes. No negative marking. Pass: 60% (90 marks) for General.

extra_sections.heading: Rajasthan-Specific GK for REET Prelims
content:
Rajasthan History & Kingdoms

Mewar Kingdom (7th century–1949): Ruled by Ranas/Maharajas (Sisodia clan). Capital: Udaipur (built by Maharaja Udai Singh II, 1559).

Mewar is famous for: Aravalli fort-towns (Chittorgarh — symbol of Rajput valor, besieged multiple times), warrior queens (Rani Padmavati), independence struggle (Maharana Pratap's resistance to Akbar, Battle of Haldighati 1576). Mewar royals were last to join British, maintaining independence longest.
REET Social Studies (L2): Mewar history, Rani Padmavati legends, Maharana Pratap, Udaipur city development.

Geography: Thar Desert & Aravalli Mountains

Thar Desert covers 60% of Rajasthan (world's 17th largest desert). Characteristics: Sandy dunes, sparse vegetation (acacia, khejri trees), extreme temperatures (50°C summer, 5°C winter), low rainfall (<25 cm annually in Jaisalmer).

Aravalli Mountains: Ancient range (1.2 billion years old), runs north-south through Rajasthan, provides water divide, forests, mineral resources (marble, slate, limestone — Rajasthan is marble capital producing 50% of India's marble). Rivers: Sutlej (northwest), Indus (boundary), Luni (internal), Chambal, Banas (tributaries of Yamuna).
EVS (L1) questions: desert animals (camel, lizards), water conservation (stepwells — traditional Rajasthani architecture), agriculture in arid regions (bajra, jowar — millet crops).

Culture: Folk Traditions & Crafts

Rajasthani music: Ghoomar dance (women's circle dance, Udaipur tradition), Kalbelia dance (performed by Kalbelia snake-charmer caste, UNESCO intangible heritage), Gair dance (male group dance). Folk singers: Manganiars (Muslim caste), Langas (hereditary musicians).

Crafts: Blue pottery (Jaipur — intricate designs), Bandhani tie-dye textiles (Jodhpur, Jaisalmer), block printing (Bagru, Ajrakhpur), camel leather, marble carving, miniature painting. Festivals: Teej (monsoon celebration), Pushkar Camel Fair (largest camel market + pilgrimage), Jaisalmer Desert Festival.
Social Studies (L2): Cultural diversity, tradition-modernity balance, artisan welfare, cultural tourism.

Economy: Agriculture in Arid Lands

Despite 60% desert coverage, Rajasthan is agrarian (65% population dependent on agriculture). Major crops: Bajra and jowar (millets, drought-resistant), cotton, oilseeds (mustard, groundnut), spices.
Rajasthan produces 15% of India's mustard (golden reserves if oil reserves fail), 12% of cotton. Pastoral economy: Camel and livestock rearing (goats, sheep) is traditional livelihood.

Mining: Marble (Rajasthan = India's marble store), sandstone (for monuments), salt (from salt lakes). Tourism economy: Jaipur (Pink City), Udaipur (Venice of East), Jodhpur (Blue City), Pushkar (pilgrimage).
EVS (L1): Water conservation in deserts, sustainable agriculture, traditional ecological knowledge (stepwells, tanks for water harvesting).

preparation_tips: No negative marking = attempt all 150 questions. This is your advantage. Never leave blanks. Even 50-50 guesses give you expected value of 0.5 marks per question (better than 0 for blank). Strategy: (1) Attempt easy questions first (75 questions in 60 min). (2) Medium questions (50 Qs in 40 min). (3) Difficult remaining (25 Qs in 50 min). (4) Final 5 minutes: verify answers.
CTET and REET overlap 80% — if preparing for CTET, appear for REET with minimal extra effort. Add Rajasthan GK (3–4 weeks) and you're ready.

Child Development (30 Qs) is high-weightage — 20% of exam. Topics: Piaget's cognitive development, attachment (Bowlby), emotional intelligence, special needs, inclusive education. These are straightforward if you have studied B.Ed content properly. Score 24+/30 here is achievable with focused revision.

Mock tests from month 2. Take 2–3 full-length mocks per week. Target: 100+/150 (67%) to be safe. Analyze each mock: Which sections took longest? Where did you make silly errors? Which topics are weak?

faqs.question: Is REET lifetime validity really permanent?
faqs.answer: Yes. As of 2021, REET validity changed to lifetime — no expiry date. This is huge advantage. Even if you clear REET today but don't get recruited immediately, your REET score remains valid forever. You can apply for recruitment exams 5, 10, even 20 years later if needed.

faqs.question: Should I target Level 1 or Level 2?
faqs.answer: Level 2 (₹36,600+ basic) pays significantly more than Level 1 (₹23,700 basic) — ₹13,000/month difference. If you have B.Ed, always target Level 2. Level 1 is only if you have D.El.Ed but not B.Ed. You can appear for both levels in same exam cycle, so do both.

faqs.question: REET passing guarantee me a job?
faqs.answer: No. REET is only eligibility gate. Actual recruitment is through separate Rajasthan 3rd Grade Teacher recruitment exam (conducted by RSMSSB). You must clear both REET (one-time) + Recruitment exam (periodic) to get appointed. REET score is valid for recruitment exams conducted anytime in future.

syllabus.subject: Child Development & Pedagogy
syllabus.topics: Child development, learning theories, pedagogy, inclusive education, assessment, NCF 2005, Rajasthan education policies

syllabus.subject: Language I (Hindi/English/Sanskrit/Urdu/Sindhi/Punjabi/Gujarati)
syllabus.topics: Grammar, comprehension, pedagogy of language teaching

syllabus.subject: Language II
syllabus.topics: Different language from Language I. Grammar, comprehension, language pedagogy.

syllabus.subject: Mathematics / Science / Social Studies
syllabus.topics: Content knowledge + pedagogy. Level 1: primary level. Level 2: upper primary level.

study_plan.tasks: REET includes Rajasthan-specific education content. Study CDP theories + Rajasthan education policies, state board curriculum, local pedagogy approaches. Language preparation.
Subject content from NCERT + Rajasthan board textbooks. Previous year papers. Focus on pedagogy questions in every section. Mock tests weekly.
Daily mocks. Revise CDP and pedagogy. Qualifying marks vary by category. No negative marking. Attempt all questions.
